worth_it_explanation: Integral Ad Science (IAS) viewability measurement is the mechanism that tells an advertiser whether a delivered impression had a reasonable opportunity to be seen. Without a defined standard in place, a campaign's viewability rate is a number with no denominator: it may reflect only the impressions IAS was able to measure, not all delivered impressions. Each check in this section is a configuration review, not a rebuild, and the settings live in IAS Signal and in the DSP or ad server integration.
intro: IAS measures viewability against the Media Rating Council (MRC) standard: 50% of pixels in view for at least 1 continuous second for display, and 50% of pixels in view for at least 2 continuous seconds for video. IAS also offers stricter custom thresholds, including time-in-view and fully on-screen metrics, through its Total Visibility product. These checks confirm that a viewability standard is defined, that it is tracked and acted on when performance falls short, and that the measured rate is not obscuring the true picture of how many delivered impressions were actually viewable.

audit_checks.title: No viewability standard is defined or applied to the campaign
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without a defined viewability standard, delivered impressions include placements that had no realistic opportunity to be seen. Budget is spent against inventory the audience never had the chance to view, and optimization signals are based on delivery counts that mix viewable and non-viewable impressions together.

educate.base:
The Media Rating Council (MRC) defines a viewable display impression as one where at least 50% of the ad's pixels are in the user's viewport for a minimum of 1 continuous second. For video, the threshold is 50% of pixels in view for at least 2 continuous seconds. These criteria represent the floor, not a goal: they establish whether the ad had a reasonable opportunity to be seen, not whether it was noticed or engaged with.

Integral Ad Science (IAS) measures viewability against the MRC standard by default, and it also offers stricter custom metrics through its Total Visibility product line, including time-in-view thresholds (e.g., 5 or 10 seconds) and fully on-screen requirements (100% of pixels in view). These custom metrics go beyond the MRC floor and can be useful when the campaign objective depends on deeper exposure.

The measured rate and the viewable rate are two separate figures in IAS reporting. The measured rate is the share of delivered impressions that IAS was able to evaluate for viewability at all. The viewable rate is the share of measured impressions that met the viewability threshold. When the measured rate is low, a high viewable rate is misleading because it describes only the impressions the system could assess, not total delivery.

A few signs suggest a viewability standard has not been defined or applied:
  • The campaign brief or media plan does not include a viewability floor or target
  • IAS Signal shows viewability data for the campaign but no viewability-based segment or threshold was applied at the DSP or placement level
  • The DSP line item has no viewability-optimized or viewability-targeted setting enabled
You may want to confirm whether the campaign brief specifies a viewability requirement before checking whether the IAS configuration reflects one.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, display and video impressions that do not meet the MRC viewability threshold deliver no exposure value against a shopper who never saw the ad. You may want to confirm that viewability floor settings are applied at the DSP line-item level before evaluating return on ad spend (ROAS).
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running awareness-stage video, the MRC two-second threshold for video is the minimum. You may want to confirm whether the campaign objective warrants a stricter time-in-view target through IAS Total Visibility, particularly when video completion rate is a campaign key performance indicator (KPI).

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Viewable Ad Impression Measurement
Open Measurement SDK from IAB Tech Lab

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal setup guides and viewability configuration documentation are available through the IAS Help Center, which requires a partner login. Contact your IAS account manager or log in at the IAS Help Center to access product guides for IAS Signal viewability dashboards and Total Visibility setup.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether the campaign has a defined viewability standard, and whether that standard is reflected in the DSP line-item settings and in IAS Signal reporting.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and navigate to the campaign or advertiser you are auditing.
Open the Viewability dashboard. Confirm that viewability data is being recorded for this campaign. If no data appears, the IAS tag may not be attached to the creatives.

Note the viewable rate (the share of measured impressions that met the viewability threshold) and the measured rate (the share of delivered impressions that IAS was able to evaluate). A low measured rate means the viewable rate reflects only a portion of total delivery.

Confirm whether a custom viewability threshold, such as time-in-view through Total Visibility, has been applied, or whether reporting reflects the default MRC threshold of 50% of pixels in view for 1 second (display) or 2 seconds (video).
Check whether a viewability floor was set at the DSP level as a line-item targeting or optimization parameter. This is separate from IAS Signal reporting and must be confirmed in the DSP interface.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether a viewability standard is documented in the campaign brief or media plan, and whether a corresponding threshold is applied in the DSP or IAS configuration. A campaign with a documented viewability floor that is applied at the line-item level passes. A campaign with no documented standard, or where IAS records viewability data but no threshold is applied at the DSP level, is a fail recorded with the campaign or line item name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record whether a viewability standard is defined in the campaign brief and whether it is applied in the DSP or IAS Signal configuration above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that no viewability standard has been defined or applied to {{site_name}}'s campaign. IAS Signal is recording viewability data, but the campaign brief does not specify a viewability floor, and no viewability threshold is applied at the DSP line-item level. Without a defined standard, delivered impressions include placements that had no realistic opportunity to be seen, and the viewable rate in IAS Signal describes only the impressions IAS was able to measure, not total delivery. {{voice_recommend}} documenting a viewability floor for each campaign type, using the MRC threshold of 50% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second for display and 2 continuous seconds for video as the baseline, and applying that threshold at the DSP line-item level so non-viewable inventory is excluded before the bid.

audit_checks.title: A viewability goal is not set, or underperformance against the goal is not acted on
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A viewability goal without a review process is a metric that accumulates without informing decisions. When IAS Signal reports a viewable rate below the campaign target and no action is taken, the campaign continues to serve against placements the team has already identified as underperforming on viewability.

educate.base:
A viewability goal is a campaign-level target, typically expressed as a viewable rate, that defines acceptable performance. It differs from a viewability floor: a floor is a technical threshold applied at the DSP or IAS level to exclude non-viewable inventory before delivery. A goal is a benchmark used to evaluate whether delivered impressions are meeting a quality standard the team has agreed on.

IAS Signal surfaces viewability rates at the campaign, line-item, and placement level. When a campaign has a defined goal, IAS Signal's reporting lets the team identify which placements or line items are pulling the rate below target and whether the gap is closing or widening over time.

The measurement value is limited if the reporting is not reviewed on a cadence and if underperformance does not lead to a concrete response. Common responses include excluding low-viewability placements from future buys, shifting budget to higher-viewability inventory, adjusting DSP line-item settings to target viewability-optimized supply, or consulting with IAS on whether stricter pre-bid segments would address the underperformance.

A few signs suggest the goal is not being acted on:
  • IAS Signal shows a viewable rate consistently below a stated goal with no corresponding change in line-item targeting or placement exclusions
  • The campaign brief specifies a viewability target but no review cadence or escalation process is documented
  • Placement-level reports in IAS Signal show persistent underperformers that appear in multiple reporting periods without being excluded
You may want to check whether the team has a scheduled review of IAS Signal viewability data tied to the campaign's flight dates.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, a viewability goal that goes unreviewed during a product launch or promotional flight means the campaign may run its full budget against partially visible inventory without a mid-flight adjustment. You may want to confirm the review cadence matches the campaign's pacing schedule.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, persistent low-viewability placements can affect quality scores and delivery efficiency over the campaign flight. You may want to confirm whether IAS Signal placement exclusions are being applied in the DSP after each review cycle.

learn_more.source_title: MRC Standards and Guidelines: Viewable Ad Impression Measurement

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal viewability benchmark data and goal-tracking configuration are available through the IAS Help Center, which requires a partner login. Contact your IAS account manager for guidance on setting campaign-level viewability benchmarks in IAS Signal.

investigate.intro: Confirm whether a viewability goal is documented for the campaign, and whether IAS Signal data shows the goal being met or whether underperformance is being identified and addressed.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and navigate to the Viewability dashboard for the campaign you are auditing.
Note the viewable rate for the current and prior reporting periods. Compare this against any stated campaign viewability goal.
Review the placement-level breakdown to identify individual placements with viewable rates significantly below the campaign average or below the stated goal.

Check whether those placements appear in multiple reporting periods without having been excluded or adjusted. Recurring underperformers that have not been addressed indicate the reporting is not feeding back into campaign management.

Confirm with the campaign team whether a formal review cadence for IAS Signal viewability data is in place, and whether low-viewability placements are being reported to the DSP or media partner for exclusion.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether a viewability goal is documented, the current viewable rate from IAS Signal, and whether low-viewability placements identified in prior reporting periods have been excluded or addressed. A campaign that documents a viewability goal, reviews IAS Signal data on a defined cadence, and applies placement exclusions based on that review passes. A campaign with a documented goal but a persistent gap between the goal and the reported viewable rate, with no evidence of corrective action, is a fail recorded with the campaign name, the stated goal, the current viewable rate, and the reporting periods reviewed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the campaign's viewability goal, the current viewable rate from IAS Signal, and whether low-viewability placements have been excluded above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s campaign has a documented viewability goal but that underperformance against that goal has not led to corrective action. IAS Signal shows a viewable rate below the campaign target across multiple reporting periods, and low-viewability placements identified in earlier reports remain active in the campaign. {{voice_recommend}} establishing a formal review cadence for IAS Signal viewability data tied to the campaign's flight schedule, and applying placement-level exclusions in the DSP after each review cycle so that placements persistently below the viewability goal are removed from active targeting.

audit_checks.title: Viewability is read in aggregate without accounting for the measured rate
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When the measured rate is low, a headline viewable rate overstates the campaign's actual viewability performance. The viewable rate reflects only impressions IAS was able to assess. Impressions that were not measured are excluded from the denominator, which makes the viewable rate look higher than the true picture of delivered inventory warrants.

educate.base:
IAS viewability reporting produces two distinct rates that are easy to conflate. The measured rate is the percentage of served impressions that IAS successfully evaluated for viewability. The viewable rate is the percentage of those measured impressions that met the viewability threshold, typically the MRC standard of 50% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second for display or 2 continuous seconds for video.

The measured rate is less than 100% because IAS cannot evaluate every impression. Some impressions are served in environments where the IAS tag cannot load, where JavaScript is disabled, or where the inventory source does not support third-party measurement. The measured rate varies by environment: open web display typically has high measurement coverage; in-app and connected television (CTV) environments historically have lower coverage, though the IAB Tech Lab's Open Measurement SDK (OM SDK) is designed to extend measurement capability across mobile apps and CTV.

When the measured rate is low, the viewable rate describes a subset of delivery. The combined metric that accounts for both is sometimes described as the 'true viewability rate': the viewable rate multiplied by the measured rate, expressed as a share of total served impressions. A campaign with a 70% viewable rate but a 50% measured rate has a true viewability rate of approximately 35% against total delivery.

A few signs suggest the measured rate is not being reviewed:
  • IAS Signal reports are reviewed for the viewable rate column only, without checking the measured rate column alongside it
  • Campaign reporting documents include a viewable rate without disclosing the corresponding measured rate
  • The measured rate has dropped materially from one reporting period to the next without an investigation into which placements or inventory sources caused the change
You may want to pull both metrics together in IAS Signal and review the measured rate at the placement and environment level, not only at the campaign total.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running display across open web and in-app environments, the measured rate may differ significantly between those two environments. You may want to review the measured rate broken out by environment in IAS Signal to confirm whether the aggregate viewable rate is being pulled down or inflated by an unmeasured segment.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser investing in CTV placements, CTV measurement coverage through third-party tags is lower than on open web. You may want to confirm whether the IAS Signal measured rate for CTV line items reflects OM SDK-based measurement or tag-based measurement, as the two have different coverage characteristics.

learn_more.source_title: Open Measurement SDK from IAB Tech Lab
MRC Standards and Guidelines: Viewable Ad Impression Measurement

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal measurement coverage reporting and environment-level measured rate breakdowns are available through the IAS Help Center, which requires a partner login. Contact your IAS account manager for guidance on reading measurement coverage data in IAS Signal.

investigate.intro: Pull both the measured rate and the viewable rate from IAS Signal, and review whether the measured rate is high enough for the viewable rate to be a reliable indicator of campaign-wide viewability performance.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and navigate to the Viewability dashboard for the campaign you are auditing.

Locate both the measured rate column and the viewable rate column. If the dashboard displays only the viewable rate by default, check whether a measured rate or measurement coverage metric is available in the report builder or a secondary dashboard view.

Note the measured rate at the campaign total level and then break it down by placement, environment, or device type if that granularity is available. A measured rate below 70% at the campaign level warrants further investigation.

Calculate an approximate true viewability rate by multiplying the viewable rate by the measured rate. Compare this figure against the headline viewable rate to understand how much of the gap is attributable to unmeasured impressions.

Identify placements or line items with measured rates significantly below the campaign average. These may be in-app or connected television (CTV) environments where tag-based measurement coverage is lower. Note whether those environments are a significant share of total delivery.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the measured rate and viewable rate from IAS Signal at the campaign level and at the environment or placement level where the measured rate varies materially. A campaign with a measured rate above 70% and a viewable rate reviewed alongside it passes. A campaign where the viewable rate is reported without the corresponding measured rate, or where the measured rate is below 70% without a documented explanation, is a note recorded with the campaign name, the measured rate, the viewable rate, and the environments contributing to low measurement coverage.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the measured rate and viewable rate from IAS Signal, broken out by environment where available, above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s campaign viewability reporting reflects the viewable rate without accounting for the measured rate. IAS Signal shows a measured rate below the threshold where the viewable rate can be taken as a reliable indicator of campaign-wide viewability performance. When a significant share of delivered impressions is not measured, the viewable rate describes only the subset IAS was able to assess, not total delivery. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing both the measured rate and the viewable rate together in IAS Signal on a consistent cadence, identifying which environments or placements are contributing to low measurement coverage, and confirming whether those environments support the IAB Tech Lab Open Measurement SDK (OM SDK) for broader coverage.
